import io
import string
import list

fn parse_ints(_ line: String) -> List<Int>
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  mut token = ""
  mut i = 0
  let n = string.len(line)
  while i < n
    match string.byte_at(line, i)
    when Some(b)
      if b == 32 or b == 9 or b == 10 or b == 13
        if not string.is_empty(token)
          match string.parse_int(token)
          when Some(v)
            result = list.push(result, v)
          when None
            ()
          end
          token = ""
        end
      else
        token = "#{token}#{string.from_byte(b)}"
      end
    when None
      ()
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  if not string.is_empty(token)
    match string.parse_int(token)
    when Some(v)
      result = list.push(result, v)
    when None
      ()
    end
  end
  result
end

fn find_pair(_ nums: List<Int>, _ t: Int) -> Option<String>
  let n = nums.len()
  mut i = 0
  mut answer: Option<String> = None
  while i < n
    mut j = i + 1
    while j < n
      match answer
      when Some(_)
        ()
      when None
        match nums.get(i)
        when Some(a)
          match nums.get(j)
          when Some(b)
            if a + b == t
              answer = Some("found=#{i},#{j}")
            end
          when None
            ()
          end
        when None
          ()
        end
      end
      j = j + 1
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  answer
end

fn main() -> Unit
  match io.read_line()
  when Some(l1)
    match io.read_line()
    when Some(l2)
      let nums = parse_ints(l1)
      match string.parse_int(string.trim(l2))
      when Some(t)
        match find_pair(nums, t)
        when Some(msg)
          println(msg)
        when None
          println("none")
        end
      when None
        println("none")
      end
    when None
      println("none")
    end
  when None
    println("none")
  end
end
